markdown:
#  Add a Partial Budget Line Item 
##  Background 
A _partial budget line item_ is a new budget line item that includes only a [budget code](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms). It is sometimes referred to as an 'unbudgeted' or 'missing' line item, because it was NOT included on the original budget. To see an example and learn more, see [What is a partial budget line item?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-a-partial-budget-line-item)
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * 'Standard' level permissions or higher on the project's Budget tool.
  * **Additional Information:**
    * You can create partial budget line items on a locked or unlocked budget. 
#####  Important
When adding and working with partial budget line items, there are some important considerations to be aware of. Before adding a partial budget line item, be sure to review [How do I work with partial budget line items?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-a-partial-budget-line-item)
  * **For companies using ERP Integrations:**
    * After adding Budget line items as detailed below, they can be exported to an integrated ERP system. See [Export a Budget to an Integrated ERP System](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/export-a-budget-to-an-integrated-erp-system).


##  Prerequisites 
Your company's Procore Administrator must complete these steps:
  * Add the Budget tool to the project. See [Add](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-project/tutorials/add-and-remove-project-tools)[ and Remove Project Tools](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-project/tutorials/add-and-remove-project-tools).
  * Complete the steps in [Create Your Company's Default Work Breakdown Structure](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/create-your-companys-default-work-breakdown-structure).
  * _(Optional)_ Complete the steps in [Create Your Project's Work Breakdown Structure](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/resource-tracking-and-unit-quantity-based-budget-setup-guide/create-your-projects-wbs).


##  Steps 
There are two ways to add partial budget line items individually or in bulk:
  * Add Line Items Individually
  * Add Line Items in Bulk


### Add Line Items Individually
  1. Navigate to the project's **Budget** tool
  2. Hover the mouse cursor over the unbudgeted line item.  
_Note_ : Procore displays a question mark (?) next to line items that are associated with unbudgeted cost code/cost type/sub job combinations.
  3. Hover the mouse cursor over the left column until a check box appears. Then place checkmark in the box.  
_Note_ : You may select as many line items as necessary.  
  
![Add Budgeted Line Items](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/bf5b87f5886e7672fa5614aa69cc9b4e/6c67ffd305cbec59fe18ada9c3debf7a/add-budgeted-line-items.png?fm=webp)  

  4. Click **Add Line Items to Budget**. 


#####  Tips
  * If the Budget is unlocked (see [Unlock a Budget](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/unlock-a-budget)), edit the Original Budget Amount inline to assign it a value.
  * You can transfer funds from another line item into this one by creating a budget change. See [Create Budget Changes](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/resource-tracking-and-project-financials-setup-guide/record-changes-on-an-internal-budget-change).


### Add Line Items in Bulk
  1. Navigate to the project's **Budget** tool.
  2. To the left of the **Description** header, place a checkmark in the box. This will select all the line items that can be added to the budget.  
  
![Bulk Add Budgeted Line Items](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/50a37ba1671dca1d762a4fd506ee6dc9/6a14cc08f8e4430eed3e21de71ae4a07/bulk-add-budgeted-line-items.png?fm=webp)  

  3. Click **Add Line Items to Budget**.


#####  Tips
  * If the Budget is unlocked (see [Unlock a Budget](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/unlock-a-budget)), edit the Original Budget Amount inline to assign it a value.
  * You can transfer funds from another line item into this one by creating a budget change. See [Create Budget Changes](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/resource-tracking-and-project-financials-setup-guide/record-changes-on-an-internal-budget-change).


## See Also
  * [Apply the View, Snapshot, Group, and Filter Options on a Budget View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/apply-the-view-snapshot-group-and-filter-options-on-a-budget-view)
  * [Delete a Budget Line Item](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/delete-a-budget-line-item)
  * [Create a Budget Modification](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/create-a-budget-modification)


A _Procore Administrator_ is a user who has 'Admin' level permissions on all of the Company level Tools in Procore. Granting a user ‘Admin’ level permissions in the Company level Directory tool automatically assigns that user ‘Admin’ permissions on all Company level tools. Also called a _Company Administrator_.
title: Add a Partial Budget Line Item
description: How to add new line items to the budget that are associated with financial line items with a cost code/cost type/sub job combination that's currently not represented in the budget.
